title: Excalidraw test
description: Make a plan of how to best be able to inject something into the excalidraw and somehow extract something from it.

title: Build SSE-driven agent watcher script
description:
Create a shell script that listens to the /api/events SSE stream and automatically launches `claude -p "/agent <id>"` whenever a new pending task appears. This replaces the manual `/agent` invocation with an always-on listener.

## Proposed approach

A bash script (`watch-agent.sh`) that:
1. Connects to `http://localhost:3001/api/events` via curl SSE
2. Parses each SSE `data:` line for task events with `"status":"pending"`
3. Extracts the task ID and runs `claude -p "/agent <id>"`
4. Waits for Claude to finish before picking up the next task (sequential, not parallel)
5. Reconnects automatically if the SSE connection drops

## Rough implementation

```bash
#!/bin/bash
API=http://localhost:3001
while true; do
  curl -s -N "$API/api/events" | while IFS= read -r line; do
    [[ "$line" == data:* ]] || continue
    JSON="${line#data: }"
    STATUS=$(echo "$JSON" | jq -r '.status // empty' 2>/dev/null)
    ID=$(echo "$JSON" | jq -r '.id // empty' 2>/dev/null)
    if [[ "$STATUS" == "pending" && -n "$ID" ]]; then
      echo "[$(date)] Picking up task $ID"
      claude -p "/agent $ID"
      echo "[$(date)] Finished task $ID"
    fi
  done
  echo "[$(date)] SSE disconnected, reconnecting in 5s..."
  sleep 5
done
```

## Questions for review
- Should this run as a systemd service on the VPS, or just a tmux/screen session?
- Should it handle multiple tasks in parallel, or strictly one at a time?
- Should there be a cooldown/rate limit between task pickups?
- Should it filter by priority (e.g. only auto-pick high/urgent)?
